releases.title: collectZ v3.11.0
releases.summary: This minor release starts the collection-health rule model. Review now separates true identifier gaps from sparse metadata, so digital/manual records are not pushed toward impossible identifiers while physical retail items can still ask for UPC/EAN when that is useful.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added a shared backend review classifier for `missing_identifier` and `sparse_metadata`.
Updated Dashboard Review with a separate Metadata lane and row-level clues.
Updated Library review filters so Dashboard can open either missing identifiers or sparse metadata directly.
Changed audio/game identifier expectations so digital rows can ask for artist, year, platform, or format metadata instead of UPC.
Kept physical audio/game rows eligible for UPC/EAN recommendations.
Updated OpenAPI and tests for the expanded review fields.

releases.title: collectZ v3.10.28
releases.summary: This patch turns the dependency PR cleanup into a documented security-maintenance process and adds GitHub CodeQL source scanning for JavaScript/TypeScript. It keeps CodeQL advisory at introduction while preserving the existing release gates for dependency audit, secret scan, image security, SBOM, runtime smoke, RBAC, browser regression, and edition boundaries.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added `.github/workflows/codeql.yml` for JavaScript/TypeScript CodeQL analysis on push, pull request, weekly schedule, and manual dispatch.
Added `docs/wiki/49-Dependency-PR-and-CI-Security-Coverage.md` with open PR dispositions and a CI security coverage map.
Updated CI/release docs to include CodeQL as an advisory source-scanning layer.
Moved the selected dependency/security backlog item out of the backlog and into the numbered roadmap.
Documented that `@zxing/library@0.23.0` remains deferred until its browser peer relationship is intentionally resolved.

releases.title: collectZ v3.10.27
releases.summary: This patch upgrades the backend Express runtime to Express 5 and removes the old Express 4-era `path-to-regexp` override so route registration, middleware, and API error handling run on the supported Express 5 router stack.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Updated backend `express` from `^4.22.2` to `^5.2.1`.
Removed the backend `path-to-regexp` override that forced the legacy `0.1.x` matcher.
Verified the Express 5 router uses its compatible `path-to-regexp` dependency.
Confirmed existing route registration, OpenAPI validation, and unit coverage still pass.

releases.title: collectZ v3.10.26
releases.summary: This patch upgrades the frontend Tailwind build pipeline to Tailwind CSS 4 and uses the dedicated Vite plugin while preserving the existing collectZ design tokens and component CSS.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Updated frontend `tailwindcss` from `^3.4.0` to `^4.3.0`.
Added `@tailwindcss/vite` and wired it into the Vite plugin list.
Replaced the old `@tailwind` CSS directives with the Tailwind 4 import plus a compatibility `@config` reference to the existing JavaScript Tailwind config.
Removed the obsolete PostCSS Tailwind/autoprefixer pipeline.
Flattened local component `@apply` recipes that chained through custom CSS classes so Tailwind 4 can compile them.

releases.title: collectZ v3.10.25
releases.summary: This patch upgrades the frontend React runtime pair to React 19 and verifies the existing app shell, library surfaces, and browser regression coverage still run against the upgraded renderer.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Updated frontend `react` from `^18.2.0` to `^19.2.6`.
Updated frontend `react-dom` from `^18.2.0` to `^19.2.6`.
Kept the existing Vite entrypoint using `react-dom/client` and `createRoot`.

releases.title: collectZ v3.10.24
releases.summary: This patch upgrades the backend validation library to Zod 4 and keeps collectZ's request-validation error shaping compatible with the new error surface.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Updated backend `zod` from `^3.22.4` to `^4.4.3`.
Updated validation middleware to read `ZodError.issues`, while retaining a fallback for older error shapes.
Kept validation failure responses in the same `{ error, details }` structure used by existing clients.

releases.title: collectZ v3.10.23
releases.summary: This patch upgrades the browser barcode decoder packages used by Capture Inbox and library lookup capture while keeping the version pair within the published ZXing peer dependency contract.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Updated `@zxing/browser` from `0.1.5` to `0.2.0`.
Updated `@zxing/library` from `0.21.3` to `0.22.0`, which is the compatible peer range required by the browser package.
Kept the newer `@zxing/library@0.23.0` PR out of this slice because the current browser package does not declare compatibility with it.

releases.title: collectZ v3.10.22
releases.summary: This patch consolidates the safe dependency and workflow updates from the open PR backlog so the remaining pull requests are easier to reason about.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Updated backend runtime dependencies for Express 4, AWS S3, Nodemailer, Helmet, Postgres, Axios, Fast XML Parser, rate limiting, and CSV parsing.
Updated frontend Vite to the current open patch release.
Updated CI workflow actions for upload-artifact and Trivy.
Left major or compatibility-sensitive PRs out of this slice for separate review.

releases.title: collectZ v3.10.21
releases.summary: This patch replaces the always-visible Cards/List buttons in shared library headers with a compact view-mode menu whose trigger reflects the current layout.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added a shared view-mode menu to the page header toolbar.
The trigger shows the active layout icon and opens a small menu with Grid view and List view options.
The selected layout is marked in the menu while preserving accessible menu semantics.
Existing search, filter, sort, and add controls remain available.

releases.title: collectZ v3.10.20
releases.summary: This patch quiets the right side of desktop library headers by making secondary controls more compact while keeping search, filter, view, sort, and add actions available.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Tightened the shared library header view toggle spacing.
Made the desktop add action icon-only while preserving accessible labels and titles.
Grouped view/sort controls more tightly so search and filter remain the primary desktop header controls.
Kept the existing mobile compact header behavior intact.
